title: Convergent ordered-sequence field-kind: non-interleaving Fugue-class CRDT over item-refs + move-CRDT position register, projected as a plain array
context: FGAP-005: ordered ref-collections need a sequence field that merges deterministically under concurrent insert/delete/move. This field-kind is one plugin into the substrate merge driver's field-merge dispatch (FGAP-004 / DEC-0004).
decision: 1) Authoritative representation: a non-interleaving sequence CRDT (Fugue-class) over item-refs; each element holds a unique, immutable position identifier minted relative to its neighbors at insert time; identifiers are densely orderable so an insert mints one identifier and mutates no neighbor; concurrent inserts into the same gap resolve to a deterministic order with no interleaving of distinct actors' runs. 2) Move as a first-class op via a position register (Kleppmann move-CRDT): an element's position is a register rewritten on move, resolved by hybrid logical clock; element identity is stable so a move never duplicates or drops the element, and two concurrent moves of the same element converge to one deterministic position. 3) Deterministic, causally-consistent resolution: the hybrid logical clock gives a replica-independent total order for same-gap insert ordering and move resolution; the same op-set in any arrival order yields the same sequence. 4) Field-aware merge driver: the substrate merge composes the CRDT op-sets / states for this field, at the field level. 5) Human-facing array projection: a sort-on-read helper emits the resolved sequence as a plain ordered ref-array for reading/editing; a hand-edit expressed as an array-position change is translated into CRDT ops through the block-api. Convergence lives in the stored representation; legibility lives in the projection. 6) Location: a declared sequence-field kind on the owning item; authoritative CRDT state carried in the owning <block>.json; the projection helper + field-aware merge driver live in the block-api.
consequences: A new declared field-kind (sequence-of-refs) recognized by schema + block-api, distinct from a plain ref-array.
CRDT state (position identifiers + position registers + HLC stamps) is stored in the owning <block>.json; the plain array becomes a read/edit projection, not the source of truth.
Plugs into the substrate merge driver (DEC-0004) step-3 field-merge dispatch as the sequence field-kind; the driver is the framework, this is the plugin.
Single-writer authoring order is reproduced identically to a plain array via sort-on-read, so existing read/edit ergonomics are preserved.
Latent until branch-pinned substrates + the substrate merge driver exist; until then it degrades to the current single-writer array behavior.
